draft.introduction: Matthew 15:21–28 records the encounter with the Canaanite woman, one of the few passages where Jesus initially refuses a request. Her persistence changes the outcome. From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: And behold, a Canaanite woman from that region came out and was crying, “Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David; my daughter is severely oppressed by a demon.” But he did not answer her a word. He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
translation.original_text: και ιδου γυνη χαναναια απο των οριων εκεινων εξελθουσα

translation.text: She said, “Yes, Lord, yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.” Then Jesus answered her, “O woman, great is your faith! Be it done for you as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.
translation.original_text: η δε ειπεν ναι κυριε και γαρ τα κυναρια εσθιει

translator_notes.note: This passage challenges any simple reading of Jesus’s mission as exclusively ethnic. The woman’s faith—not her lineage—secures the miracle, anticipating the universal mission of Matthew 28:19.
